description: Legal document. Partnership document. Dating: 1300-1350 (per Goitein). Mentions two partners, Abū al-Ḥasan b. Abū al-Faraj and Khalaf b. Ḥasan, who withdrew from a group involved in banking because some of the partners had loaned funds without the permission of Abū al-Ḥasan. Apparently Abū l-Ḥasan was in charge of the workplace but visited only periodically. The withdrawal appears to have taken place six years before this document was written and the account with Abū al-Ḥasan was settled. The partnership likely charged interest on some of its loans, since the clients include Muslims and Christians (e.g., a Coptic clergyman from Barbaya). (Information from Lieberman, "A Partnership Culture," 234) Also mentions a number of government officials, including Jalāl al-Dawla, head of the dār al-ṭirāz.
description_he: מסמך משפטי. מסמך שותפות. תאריך: 1300–1350 (לפי גויטיין). שני שותפים, אבו אל-חסן ב. אבו אל-פרג' וח'לף ב. חסן, פרשו מקבוצה עסקת בנקאות כי חלק מהשותפים הלוו כספים ללא אישורם
